perturbation.original_text: 2. The total sum of non-paid invoices (estimated to be approximately $80,000) payable to Marv Enterprises, LLC, previously owed by Premier to Marv up until the effective date of this Agreement, payable in total by no later than July 20, 2020.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. The total sum of non-paid invoices (estimated to be approximately $80,000) payable to Marv Enterprises, LLC, previously owed by Premier to Marv up until the effective date of this Agreement, payable in total within 10 business days of THI recieving payment from Premier for the same invoices.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the due date for the payment of outstanding invoices. Originally, the invoices were due by July 20, 2020. Now, the payment is due within 10 business days of THI receiving payment from Premier. This contradicts the fixed deadline and creates uncertainty because it links the payment deadline to an uncertain event (Premier paying THI) with no fixed final date. This creates uncertainty in enforcement, as the fixed due date no longer applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree and stipulate that each and every term and condition contained in this Agreement is material, and that each and every term and condition may be reasonably accomplished within the time limitations, and in the manner set forth in this Agreement. The Parties agree and stipulate that time is of the essence with respect to compliance with each and every item set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment due date from a fixed date (July 20, 2020) to a conditional date (10 business days after THI receives payment from Premier), which contradicts the contradicted text stating "time is of the essence with respect to compliance with each and every item set forth in this Agreement." The change introduces uncertainty and makes the original, strict deadline unenforceable, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, leading to uncertainty in which terms should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: If THI does not make the obligatory payments as stated in 2(b) by the dates stated, the Exclusive License will revert back to Premier.
perturbation.changed_text: If THI does not make the obligatory payments as stated in 2(b) within 30 days of notification of non-payment by Marv, the Exclusive License will revert back to Premier.
perturbation.explanation: By altering the clause regarding the reversion of the Exclusive License, this modification introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, the reversion occurred if THI did not make payments by the stated dates in section 2(b). Now, the reversion occurs if THI does not make payments within 30 days of notification of non-payment by Marv. Since the dates in 2(b) are still listed, and the new condition introduces a 30 day after notification period, the uncertainty lies on what is being enforced. This creates uncertainty in enforcement, as the initial 'dates stated' may create confusion for the new 30 day deadline.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree and stipulate that each and every term and condition contained in this Agreement is material, and that each and every term and condition may be reasonably accomplished within the time limitations, and in the manner set forth in this Agreement. The Parties agree and stipulate that time is of the essence with respect to compliance with each and every item set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a 30-day grace period after notification of non-payment, which contradicts the original 'time is of the essence' clause in the contradicted text. The original text emphasizes strict adherence to deadlines, while the changed text allows for a delay, creating an inconsistency in the enforcement of payment obligations. This creates a direct contradiction and the uncertainty of the new condition creates confusion for the initial 'dates stated' listed in 2(b).

perturbation.original_text: 1. $750,000, payable to THI in total by no later than June 20 , 2020. The payment will be for developing fluorescently-conjugated antibodies in the Field of Covid-19 Treatments.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. $750,000, payable to THI, split into three installments: $250,000 by June 20, 2020, $250,000 by July 20, 2020, and $250,000 upon completion of phase one trials. The payment will be for developing fluorescently-conjugated antibodies in the Field of Covid-19 Treatments.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation changes the payment schedule for the $750,000 payable to THI. Initially, it was a lump sum due by June 20, 2020. The revised clause splits the payment into three installments, with the final installment contingent on the 'completion of phase one trials.' This contradicts the original 'no later than June 20, 2020' deadline and introduces a new, ambiguous deadline dependent on a future event, causing uncertainty about the total amount due by June 20, 2020. The lack of clarity around the 'completion of phase one trials' can lead to disputes over when the final installment is actually due, undermining the contract's original definiteness regarding payment deadlines.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree and stipulate that each and every term and condition contained in this Agreement is material, and that each and every term and condition may be reasonably accomplished within the time limitations, and in the manner set forth in this Agreement. The Parties agree and stipulate that time is of the essence with respect to compliance with each and every item set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a single payment due by June 20, 2020. The changed text introduces three installments, with the final installment dependent on the 'completion of phase one trials.' This directly contradicts the original deadline and the stipulation that time is of the essence. The contradicted text emphasizes that all terms should be reasonably accomplished within the specified time limitations, creating a direct contradiction. The changed text makes it unclear when the total payment should be completed.
